form_description: Structured questions to define a software product concept, validate ideas, and develop a pitch. Covers problem, users, validation, solution, market, business model, go-to-market, and risks.

sections.name: problem_space
sections.title: Problem Space
sections.description: What problem exists and why it matters

sections.name: target_users
sections.title: Target Users
sections.description: Who experiences this problem and will use your solution

sections.name: validation
sections.title: Customer Validation
sections.description: Evidence that the problem and solution resonate

sections.name: solution
sections.title: Solution & Approach
sections.description: What you're building and how it works

sections.name: competitive
sections.title: Competitive Landscape
sections.description: What alternatives exist and why you're different

sections.name: mvp_scope
sections.title: MVP & Roadmap
sections.description: What you'll ship first and what comes next

sections.name: team
sections.title: Team & Resources
sections.description: Who's building this and what you need

sections.name: risks
sections.title: Risks & Open Questions
sections.description: What could go wrong and what you don't know yet

questions.question_text: What problem are you solving? Describe it in one sentence.
questions.helper_text: The elevator pitch. Clear, specific, jargon-free.

questions.question_text: Why does this problem exist? What's the root cause?
questions.helper_text: Dig into the underlying dynamics. Why hasn't it been solved?

questions.question_text: What happens if the problem isn't solved? What's the cost of inaction?
questions.helper_text: Economic cost, opportunity cost, pain points that persist.

questions.question_text: What insight or thesis drives your approach? What do you believe that others don't?
questions.helper_text: Your unfair insight. The 'aha' that makes your solution possible or better.

questions.question_text: Who is your primary target user? Be specific about role, context, and needs.
questions.helper_text: Not 'everyone.' Specific personas with specific needs.

questions.question_text: Who are your secondary users? How do their needs differ?
questions.helper_text: Other stakeholders who benefit or participate.

questions.question_text: What does your target user do today to solve this problem?
questions.helper_text: Current workarounds, manual processes, competing products.

questions.question_text: What would make your target user switch to your solution?
questions.helper_text: The triggering event or compelling reason to change behavior.

questions.question_text: Have you talked to potential customers? What did they say?
questions.helper_text: Direct quotes are gold. 'I interviewed 5 homeowners and they said...' Include skepticism too.

questions.question_text: What evidence do you have that people will pay for this?
questions.helper_text: Pre-orders, letters of intent, survey results, competitor revenue, adjacent market data.

questions.question_text: What's the riskiest assumption you're making? How could you test it cheaply?
questions.helper_text: The thing that if wrong, kills the idea. Propose a quick experiment.

questions.question_text: Describe your solution in plain language. What does the user do and what happens?
questions.helper_text: Walk through the core user flow. No technical jargon.

questions.question_text: What is the core value proposition? Why is this better than alternatives?
questions.helper_text: Faster? Cheaper? More accurate? More accessible? Different market entirely?

questions.question_text: What is explicitly OUT of scope? What won't you build?
questions.helper_text: Equally important as what's in. Shows focus and understanding.

questions.question_text: Who are your main competitors? Direct and indirect.
questions.helper_text: Direct = same solution. Indirect = different solution to same problem.

questions.question_text: Why do competitors fail to fully solve the problem?
questions.helper_text: Their weaknesses are your opportunity.

questions.question_text: What's your unfair advantage or moat?
questions.helper_text: Expertise, data, relationships, technology - what's hard to replicate?

questions.question_text: What is the Total Addressable Market (TAM)? How did you calculate it?
questions.helper_text: Everyone who could theoretically use this. Show your math.

questions.question_text: What is your Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM)? Who can you realistically reach?
questions.helper_text: Geographic, demographic, or segment constraints. Be realistic.

questions.question_text: What market trends support this opportunity? Why now?
questions.helper_text: Technology changes, regulatory shifts, cultural trends, economic factors.

questions.question_text: How will you make money? Describe your revenue model.
questions.helper_text: Subscription, transaction fee, licensing, freemium, enterprise, consulting, etc.

questions.question_text: Who pays? Is it the user or someone else?
questions.helper_text: User, employer, advertiser, data buyer - follow the money.

questions.question_text: What's the pricing? How did you arrive at it?
questions.helper_text: Competitor benchmarks, value-based, cost-plus? Show your thinking.

questions.question_text: What are the unit economics? Customer acquisition cost, lifetime value, margins?
questions.helper_text: Even rough estimates show you've thought about profitability.

questions.question_text: How will you acquire your first 10 customers?
questions.helper_text: Personal network, cold outreach, content, partnerships? Be specific.

questions.question_text: How will you scale to 100 and then 1000 customers?
questions.helper_text: What works at 10 often doesn't work at 1000. What's the scalable channel?

questions.question_text: What partnerships or distribution channels could accelerate growth?
questions.helper_text: Integrations, referral partners, industry associations, influencers.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum viable product? What ships first?
questions.helper_text: The smallest thing that delivers core value and tests key assumptions.

questions.question_text: What are the must-have features vs. nice-to-have?
questions.helper_text: Ruthless prioritization. What can wait for v2?

questions.question_text: What's the roadmap? What milestones with what dates?
questions.helper_text: 3-month, 6-month, 12-month view. Be specific about deliverables.

questions.question_text: What metrics will indicate you're on the right track?
questions.helper_text: User metrics, revenue, engagement - how will you know it's working?

questions.question_text: What is the high-level technical architecture?
questions.helper_text: Frontend, backend, data storage, key integrations. Diagram in words.

questions.question_text: What are the key technical challenges or unknowns?
questions.helper_text: What's technically hard? What needs R&D or prototyping?

questions.question_text: What's the build vs. buy strategy? What will you use off-the-shelf?
questions.helper_text: Where does custom code create value vs. commodity infrastructure?

questions.question_text: Who is on the team? What relevant experience do they bring?
questions.helper_text: Founders, key hires, advisors. Why is this team uniquely suited?

questions.question_text: What skills or roles are missing? How will you fill them?
questions.helper_text: Technical, sales, domain expertise - what gaps exist?

questions.question_text: What resources do you need? Time, money, equipment, partnerships?
questions.helper_text: Be specific about what you're asking for and what it enables.

questions.question_text: What are the biggest risks to this product's success?
questions.helper_text: Technical, market, competitive, execution, regulatory - all types.

questions.question_text: What assumptions are you making that haven't been validated?
questions.helper_text: User willingness to pay, technical feasibility, market size - what's unproven?

questions.question_text: What's your Plan B if key assumptions prove wrong?
questions.helper_text: Pivot options, fallback positions, kill criteria.

questions.question_text: What open questions need answers before you can proceed?
questions.helper_text: Decisions pending, research needed, stakeholder input required.

business_directions.name: B2C Product
business_directions.description: Direct to consumer - app store, subscriptions, ads

business_directions.name: B2B SaaS
business_directions.description: Business customers - enterprise sales, per-seat licensing

business_directions.name: Consulting + Software
business_directions.description: Services wrapped with tools - consulting fees plus software license

business_directions.name: Platform/Marketplace
business_directions.description: Connect buyers and sellers - transaction fees, network effects
